identificationModule.briefTitle: Epirubicin Versus Docetaxel Plus Cyclophosphamide in Lymph Node Negative, ER-positive, Her2-negative Breast Cancer
identificationModule.officialTitle: Comparison the Safety and Efficacy of Epirubicin Plus Cyclophosphamide (EC)Versus Docetaxel Plus Cyclophosphamide (TC) in Lymph Node Negative, ER Positive, Her2 Negative Breast Cancer Patients as Adjuvant Chemotherapy

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to compare the safety and efficacy of epirubicin plus cyclophosphamide versus docetaxel plus cyclophosphamide in lymph node negative, estrogen receptor (ER) positive, human epithelial growth factor receptor 2 (HER2) negative breast cancer patients as adjuvant chemotherapy.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: This is a non-inferiority study. According to previous study results, the investigators hypothesize the incidence of grade 3-4 neutropenia is 40%, with a α of 0.05 and β of 0.8, a sample size of 264 cases if needed.

interventions.name: Epirubicin
interventions.description: epirubicin, 90mg per square meter, every 3 weeks, day 1

interventions.name: Docetaxel
interventions.description: docetaxel, 75mg per square meter, every 3 weeks, day 1

interventions.name: cyclophosphamide
interventions.description: cyclophosphamide, 600mg per square meter, every 3 weeks, day 1

primaryOutcomes.measure: number of participants with grade 3-4 neutropenia assessed by CTCAE v4.0
primaryOutcomes.description: number of participants with neutrophil count less than 1000 per milliliter

secondaryOutcomes.measure: number of participants with grade 3-4 leukopenia assessed by CTCAE v4.0
secondaryOutcomes.description: number of participants with leukocyte count less than 2000 per milliliter

secondaryOutcomes.measure: number of participants with febrile neutropenia assessed by CTCAE v4.0
secondaryOutcomes.description: number of participants with neutropenia count less than 1000 per milliliter and temperature highe than 38.5℃
number of participants with any locoregional recurrence, contralateral breast cancer or distant metastasis
number of participans died from all cause

overallOfficials.name: Li Zhu, doctor
overallOfficials.affiliation: Shanghai Jiao Tong University School of Medicine affiliated Ruijin Hospital

ipdSharingStatementModule.description: There is no plan to share IPD
